title: Prologis: Logistics real estate market in ‘unprecedented territory'
text: Logistics warehouse operator Prologis Inc. sees the market for space remaining tight for some time. On its first-quarter earnings call with analysts Tuesday, management said available supply in the market is still at all-time lows.

title: Prologis, Inc. (PLD) CEO Hamid Moghadam on Q1 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Prologis, Inc. (NYSE:PLD ) Q1 2022 Earnings Conference Call April 19, 2022 12:00 PM ET Company Participants Tracy Ward - Managing Director, Investor Relations Tim Arndt - Chief Financial Officer Tom Olinger - Outgoing Chief Financial Officer Hamid Moghadam - Chairman & Chief Executive Officer Gary Anderson - Chief Operating Officer Chris Caton - Managing Director, Global Strategy & Analytics Gene Reilly - Chief Investment Officer Conference Call Participants Jamie Feldman - Bank of America Michael Goldsmith - UBS Ki Bin Kim - Truist Steve Sakwa - Evercore ISI Caitlin Burrows - Goldman Sachs Manny Korchman - Citigroup Blaine Heck - Wells Fargo Nick Yulico - Scotiabank Ronald Kamdem - Morgan Stanley Mike Mueller - JPMorgan Derek Johnston - Deutsche Bank David Rodgers - Baird Andrew Rosivach - Wolfe Research John Kim - BMO Capital Markets Vince Tibone - Green Street Anthony Powell - Barclays Michael Bilerman - Citigroup Michael Carroll - RBC Capital Markets Operator Ladies and gentlemen, thank you for standing by. title: Prologis (PLD) Beats Q1 FFO Estimates, Issues Upbeat View
text: Prologis (PLD) puts up a decent show in the first quarter on robust activity and rent growth. It also issues an upbeat 2022 outlook.

title: Prologis (PLD) Q1 FFO Surpass Estimates
text: Prologis (PLD) delivered FFO and revenue surprises of 0.93% and 0.94%, respectively, for the quarter ended March 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Prologis ups guidance as warehouse market remains tight
text: Logistics real estate investment trust Prologis Inc. beat first-quarter expectations and raised full-year guidance on Tuesday. Core funds from operations (FFO) of $1.09 per share outpaced analysts' expectations by 2 cents and were 12 cents higher than the year-ago period.

title: Prologis stock rallies after earnings beat, raised outlook amid 'record demand'
text: Shares of Prologis Inc. PLD, +0.18% rallied 2.5% in premarket trading Tuesday, after the logistics-focused real estate investment trust reported first-quarter earnings that beat expectations, and raised its full-year outlook, citing "record demand" amid continued supply-chain challenges. Net income rose to $1.15 billion, or $1.54 a share, from $366 million, or 49 cents a share, in the year-ago period.
